message:
test(codec): ft-e1emx parameterized PDU conformance harness with tail-padding + Option tag-byte regression guard

Lifts the existing 10 individual per-PDU roundtrip tests into a single
parameterized conformance harness driven by a (label, Pdu) matrix, per
the /testing-conformance-harnesses skill. The contract checked against
every entry:

  1. canonical encode → decode equality
  2. encode determinism (encode-decode-encode is byte-stable)
  3. tail-padded decode robustness — extra trailing bytes (0x00, 0xFF×16,
     "GARBAGE_TAIL") must not corrupt the decoded PDU because the
     length-prefixed framing tells the decoder when to stop
  4. all three CompressionMode variants (Auto / Never / Always) decode
     to the same logical PDU

Matrix covers PDUs IDs 63-72: MoveFloatingPane, SetFloatingPaneZ,
ToggleFloatingPane, RemoveFloatingPane, SwapToLayout, SetLayoutCycle
(empty + non-empty), CycleStack (forward + backward), SelectStackPane,
UpdatePaneConstraints (all-none / all-some / mixed).

Adds a targeted skip_serializing_if regression guard
(`update_pane_constraints_options_emit_tag_bytes`) that asserts None
and Some encodings produce strictly different lengths, and Some > None.
This is the bug class documented in lib.rs:1204-1228 — varbincode is a
positional binary format and `skip_serializing_if` on Option<T> would
silently misalign the decoder. The guard surfaces the regression on
encode rather than waiting for downstream fields to corrupt.

120/120 codec lib tests pass.

Closes ft-e1emx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -93,7 +93,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-7ojbj","title":"rate_limit_tracker: parse_retry_after can produce Duration that panics Instant + cooldown","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/rate_limit_tracker.rs:441-473` `parse_retry_after` has no upper bound on the parsed duration.

Plain-number path at :452-454:
```rust
if let Ok(secs) = text.parse::<u64>() {
    return Some(Duration::from_secs(secs));
}
```

'N unit' path at :458-469 has a `checked_mul` guard, but the plain number path returns `Duration::from_secs(u64::MAX)` untouched (u64::MAX secs ≈ 584 billion years).

The returned Duration flows into `PaneRateLimitState::record_event` at :164:
```rust
let expiry = event.detected_at + event.cooldown;
```

`Instant + Duration` panics when the sum overflows the platform's underlying representation. On macOS/Linux Instant is backed by clock_gettime timespec/uptime — adding ~584B years to a fresh Instant will overflow.

# Reachability

`retry_after_text` reaches record_event from pattern detection output. `trigger_retry_after` (workflows/builtin_workflows.rs) extracts the string from agent-emitted rate-limit messages. A malformed provider response or adversarial agent output ('Retry-After: 18446744073709551615') would panic the tracker on record.

# Existing tests

`parse_retry_after_overflow_returns_none` at :515-518 covers the 'N unit' checked_mul path with 'u64::MAX hours', but NOT the plain-number path with 'u64::MAX'. That path currently returns `Some` unclamped.

# Fix

Clamp parse_retry_after to a sane max (24h ceiling — legitimate provider Retry-After values never exceed 1h in practice, and any longer wait is a signal to bail rather than block the tracker). Apply the cap uniformly to plain-number and 'N unit' paths. Also clamp DEFAULT_COOLDOWN_SECS fallback if it ever grows.

Add regression test: parse_retry_after_plain_u64_max_is_clamped asserts the plain-number path returns a Duration ≤ the cap, not None and not u64::MAX.

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires adversarial/malformed provider input to trigger. Tracker is behind rate_limit_tracker.lock() in workflows/builtin_workflows.rs so the panic takes down the workflow task but is scoped to one pane's rate-limit processing. Fix is trivial: one `.min(MAX_COOLDOWN)` call plus one test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:08.211722Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213664Z","close_reason":"Landed in 7998d1a4 (fix(rate_limit_tracker): clamp parsed Retry-After at MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7quzz","title":"headless_mux_server: server_config_serde test missing max_peers after ft-ry224 added the field","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/headless_mux_server.rs:1116 has a ServerConfig struct literal in test server_config_serde that pre-dates ft-ry224's addition of max_peers: u32 (line 120). The test now fails to compile with E0063. Trivial: add max_peers: <value> to the literal.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:43:29.638577Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:51:45.829569Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:51:45.829266Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7s0vw","title":"event_id::detect_clock_anomaly overflows on prev + future_skew_threshold","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_id.rs:205\` computes
\`prev + future_skew_threshold\` without overflow protection. In release
builds u64 wraps silently; in debug builds the unchecked add panics.
Either way, a pathological or adversarial \`prev\` value breaks the
future-skew branch.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`detect_clock_anomaly(current, prev, threshold)\` where
   \`prev + threshold\` overflows \`u64\`.
2. Easy reach: replay/import flows feeding \`recorded_at_ms = u64::MAX\` from
   a corrupt fixture, fuzzer, or attacker-controlled input. Also reachable
   via legitimate-but-distant clock sources (UNIX epoch micros around year
   586,524 if anyone reaches that, or any monotonic clock that has been
   bumped by a process bug into the high u64 range).
3. **Release build:** \`prev + threshold\` wraps to a tiny value. The
   condition \`current > tiny_value\` is now true for almost any \`current\`,
   so the function returns \`is_anomaly: true\` for every subsequent valid
   timestamp — flooding telemetry with false-positive "future skew"
   reports and potentially triggering downstream remediation that should
   not have fired.
4. **Debug build:** unchecked add panics (\`attempt to add with overflow\`),
   killing whatever thread was processing the recorder pipeline.
5. \`ClockAnomalyTracker::observe\` always updates the baseline
   ("Updates the baseline regardless") — so a single malicious or
   transiently-high \`current\` value sticks around and continues to
   poison overflow checks against subsequent observations.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer in release; thread-killing panic in debug.
- Adversary-reachable via fixture/replay/fuzz inputs.
- Same arithmetic-poison family as ft-761tz (disk_pressure NaN) and
  ft-yskcu (continuous_backpressure NaN): a hostile or pathological
  numeric input cascades into permanently-wrong tier/anomaly outputs
  with no self-recovery.

## Fix sketch

1. Replace \`prev + future_skew_threshold\` with \`prev.checked_add(threshold)\`.
2. On overflow, treat as "no future-skew anomaly" — we cannot prove a
   timestamp is too far in the future relative to a near-MAX baseline,
   so the only sound default is \"don't flag\". Same disposition as
   ft-761tz: when the math itself becomes unreliable, fail toward
   silence rather than toward false positives.
3. Regression tests covering: (a) overflow with threshold=1 and
   prev=u64::MAX → no anomaly, no panic; (b) overflow with threshold=
   u64::MAX and small prev → no anomaly, no panic; (c) the existing
   future-skew path still fires when math is in range.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_id.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. \`ClockAnomalyTracker::observe\` baseline-update behavior is
intentional and out of scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:30:32.110899Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:36:43.255991Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:36:43.255609Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 4431483f: detect_clock_anomaly now uses prev.checked_add(future_skew_threshold), failing silent on overflow rather than panicking (debug) or wrapping (release). 5 regression tests added covering (current=prev=u64::MAX, threshold=1), (huge threshold), (boundary u64::MAX × u64::MAX), (in-range path still fires), and (tracker survives MAX baseline). All 53 event_id tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target. Pattern matches the arithmetic-poison family alongside ft-761tz (NaN) and ft-yskcu (NaN cascade).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-7tq4z","title":"[HIGH] SCHEMA_SQL drift: fresh DBs skip v24 policy_denied_audit table (storage.rs:4440-4446 sets user_version=SCHEMA_VERSION without running migrations)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:09.392483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T02:06:21.696366Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","schema","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3641,"issue_id":"ft-7tq4z","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Discovered while writing ft-cro2u

Pre-existing bug surfaced by the new audit-persist test in ft-cro2u.
The fresh-DB initialization path at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:4440-4446` is:

    if current == 0 && needs_init {
        conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)?;
        set_user_version(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
        record_migration(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION, "Initial schema")?;
        ensure_ft_meta(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
        return Ok(());
    }

It runs the static `SCHEMA_SQL` blob and stamps the user_version
directly to `SCHEMA_VERSION` (currently 24) — but `SCHEMA_SQL` does
NOT include the `policy_denied_audit` table that landed in migration
v24. New databases never get that table created.

# Repro

A brand-new SQLite file opened via `StorageHandle::new(path)` has
`user_version = 24` (per `SCHEMA_VERSION`) but
`SELECT * FROM policy_denied_audit` returns
`SqliteFailure: no such table: policy_denied_audit`.

# Impact

* Every MCP policy-deny on a fresh DB silently fails the audit-persist
  step (`persist_mcp_policy_denial_async` warn-logs and continues).
  Operators get a denied response in tracing but no row in
  `policy_denied_audit` to query later.
* This affects new installs / new test harnesses / fresh CI workspaces.
  Existing user DBs that walked through migrations (v0 → v1 → … → v24)
  DID get the table because v24's `up_sql` ran for them.

# Two fixes

1. **Append CREATE TABLE policy_denied_audit (and its two indexes) to
   SCHEMA_SQL.** Each `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` is idempotent so
   existing migrated DBs won't double-create. Cheap. Makes fresh DBs
   match the migrated-up DBs.
2. **Run migrations on the fresh-DB path.** Replace the early-return
   with `conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)?; set_user_version(conn, 0)?;
   run_migrations(conn, 0)?;` so every migration helper applies. More
   thorough but also runs every helper for tables SCHEMA_SQL already
   created — harmless idempotently, but extra work.

(1) is the smaller change and matches the documented invariant that
SCHEMA_SQL == "the canonical schema at the latest version". Fix (2)
would be the more general structural answer.

# Workaround in test scope (ft-cro2u)

The new `wait_for_tool_persists_policy_denial_audit_when_storage_is_attached`
test pre-seeds the table with a direct rusqlite connection before
invoking the tool, with an inline comment pointing at this bead. When
this bead lands the workaround block can be deleted.

# Out of scope

Other v24+ migrations should also be audited for SCHEMA_SQL drift —
this might not be the only orphan.","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:32Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-7tq4z","title":"[HIGH] SCHEMA_SQL drift: fresh DBs skip v24 policy_denied_audit table (storage.rs:4440-4446 sets user_version=SCHEMA_VERSION without running migrations)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:09.392483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T02:16:44.933511Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T02:16:44.933247Z","close_reason":"92cd7452 fixes the SCHEMA_SQL drift by collapsing the two current==0 branches in initialize_schema into a single path through run_v0_init_in_transaction. The truly-fresh DB case now goes through repair (no-op when no tables exist) → SCHEMA_SQL → run_migrations(0) → ensure_ft_meta — same atomicity contract as ft-k542h/c06b230a. The buggy early-return that ran SCHEMA_SQL and stamped user_version=SCHEMA_VERSION directly (skipping run_migrations entirely) is deleted; needs_init is preserved for other callers (status reporters/diagnostics). Three test changes: (1) NEW fresh_db_init_creates_policy_denied_audit_via_v24_migration pins the regression — fresh DB has user_version==SCHEMA_VERSION AND the v24 policy_denied_audit table with all documented columns AND a record_policy_denial_audit_sync insert succeeds; (2) UPDATED schema_version_audit_trail_recorded asserts the new invariant (count>=1, max(version)==SCHEMA_VERSION, every version in 1..=SCHEMA_VERSION with non-null applied_at) instead of the old hardcoded count==1 / 'Initial schema'; (3) UPDATED idempotent_init_preserves_version compares snapshots (count_after_first==count_after_second) instead of hardcoding 1 — robust against MIGRATIONS growing. CLEANUP: ft-cro2u's wait_for_tool_persists test no longer needs the v24 CREATE TABLE workaround. 188 storage::tests pass via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target (3 unrelated recorder_storage frankensqlite gate failures pre-exist this commit).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","schema","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3641,"issue_id":"ft-7tq4z","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Discovered while writing ft-cro2u

Pre-existing bug surfaced by the new audit-persist test in ft-cro2u.
The fresh-DB initialization path at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:4440-4446` is:

    if current == 0 && needs_init {
        conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)?;
        set_user_version(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
        record_migration(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION, "Initial schema")?;
        ensure_ft_meta(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
        return Ok(());
    }

It runs the static `SCHEMA_SQL` blob and stamps the user_version
directly to `SCHEMA_VERSION` (currently 24) — but `SCHEMA_SQL` does
NOT include the `policy_denied_audit` table that landed in migration
v24. New databases never get that table created.

# Repro

A brand-new SQLite file opened via `StorageHandle::new(path)` has
`user_version = 24` (per `SCHEMA_VERSION`) but
`SELECT * FROM policy_denied_audit` returns
`SqliteFailure: no such table: policy_denied_audit`.

# Impact

* Every MCP policy-deny on a fresh DB silently fails the audit-persist
  step (`persist_mcp_policy_denial_async` warn-logs and continues).
  Operators get a denied response in tracing but no row in
  `policy_denied_audit` to query later.
* This affects new installs / new test harnesses / fresh CI workspaces.
  Existing user DBs that walked through migrations (v0 → v1 → … → v24)
  DID get the table because v24's `up_sql` ran for them.

# Two fixes

1. **Append CREATE TABLE policy_denied_audit (and its two indexes) to
   SCHEMA_SQL.** Each `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` is idempotent so
   existing migrated DBs won't double-create. Cheap. Makes fresh DBs
   match the migrated-up DBs.
2. **Run migrations on the fresh-DB path.** Replace the early-return
   with `conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)?; set_user_version(conn, 0)?;
   run_migrations(conn, 0)?;` so every migration helper applies. More
   thorough but also runs every helper for tables SCHEMA_SQL already
   created — harmless idempotently, but extra work.

(1) is the smaller change and matches the documented invariant that
SCHEMA_SQL == "the canonical schema at the latest version". Fix (2)
would be the more general structural answer.

# Workaround in test scope (ft-cro2u)

The new `wait_for_tool_persists_policy_denial_audit_when_storage_is_attached`
test pre-seeds the table with a direct rusqlite connection before
invoking the tool, with an inline comment pointing at this bead. When
this bead lands the workaround block can be deleted.

# Out of scope

Other v24+ migrations should also be audited for SCHEMA_SQL drift —
this might not be the only orphan.","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-7ycug","title":"[review][LOW] ea7f89f9 GUI test coverage: tests assert type stability, not rendering correctness","description":"ft-1memj.28 commit ea7f89f9 adds 5 GUI unit tests across crates/frankenterm-gui/src/{glyphcache,quad,overlay/quickselect}.rs. Audit finding: tests pass superficial coverage bar but leave real rendering edges uncovered.

Specifics:

1. glyphcache.rs test_atlas_rect_lookup and atlas_rect_lookup_separates_distinct_cached_colors verify that cached_color memoizes and that distinct colors get distinct origins. That's a hash-map property test, not an atlas-rect-lookup test. Zero coverage for:
   - Atlas-full path (OutOfTextureSpace branch in glyphcache).
   - 2x HiDPI scale — cached_color takes only (color, alpha), no DPI param; glyph rasterization at 2x scale is untested.
   - Unicode wide-width / emoji / combining marks — glyphcache has separate code paths for these (see Underline + combining handling in the same file).
   - Atlas eviction under pressure.

2. overlay/quickselect.rs extracts two helpers (merge_dirty_results, clear_rendered_dirty_result) and tests them. clear_rendered_dirty_result is a one-line wrapper around RangeSet::remove with zero added logic — the test is effectively 'does RangeSet::remove work'. merge_dirty_results test is legitimate coverage of the intersect+clip behavior. But neither exercises:
   - sequence-number ordering of dirty merges during active search.
   - dirty-row propagation when search_row changes (the line 611/705 call sites interact with pattern-match highlighting).

3. quad.rs test_cell_vertex_generation overlaps with the existing quad_propagates_colors_* test two asserts below it. Both call set_position + set_texture_discrete and check vertex coordinates. Incremental coverage value is near zero.

Verdict: coverage-metric churn, not regression-catching tests. The ft-1memj.28 bead was 'add gui unit test coverage' so the commit technically closes it, but the tests would not have caught historical GUI bugs (atlas overflow, HiDPI scaling, wide-glyph clipping, dirty-row propagation during search).

Severity: LOW — not a bug, but a test-quality finding to flag for future test passes. Not recommending a fix commit here; file for visibility when the next ft-1memj.x GUI test pass lands.

Remote verification ran twice via rch and reached real compilation, but both Linux workers failed before test execution because the required CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang path does not exist there during openssl-sys setup.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-84ulk","title":"workflow runner releases pane lock on pre-start cx cancellation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:07:27.230187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:17:04.275236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:17:04.274845Z","close_reason":"Released pane lock and marked execution failed when run_workflow_with_cx sees a pre-start cancelled child context; added regression coverage for the persisted-record + lock cleanup path.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-87tdq","title":"[testing][metamorphic] patterns.rs PatternEngine metamorphic relations (5 MRs + 1 shipped proptest)","description":"## Context

Applies /testing-metamorphic methodology to `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` (`PatternEngine::detect`).

An existing metamorphic suite `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan.rs` covers the fast-path byte scanner `pattern_trigger::TriggerScanner` (idempotence, empty-input neutrality, prefix-shift invariance, concat subadditivity, case-insensitive equality, locate bounds). The higher-level rule-based `PatternEngine` at patterns.rs:2196 is NOT covered.

## Metamorphic relations for PatternEngine::detect

**MR1 — Idempotence (SHIPPED in this bead)**: calling `engine.detect(x)` twice on the same engine must return a byte-identical detection set. A regression in engine state (e.g. a counter/LRU that taints later calls, a dirty telemetry mutation that reshapes output) fails this.

**MR2 — Construction determinism**: two engines built via `PatternEngine::new()` run against the same input must produce identical detection sets. Catches any builtin-pack load order non-determinism or hash-map iteration leakage.

**MR3 — Empty input neutrality**: `engine.detect("")` returns an empty vec. Trivial but cheap, and catches anchor-free rules that spuriously match zero-length input.

**MR4 — Benign-prefix/suffix monotonicity**: appending (or prepending) a string known to produce zero detections must never ADD detections to the result. Formally: if `detect(b) == []` then `detect(x) ⊆ detect(x + b)`. Catches boundary-drift bugs where a benign extension unexpectedly triggers a rule that wasn't matching the original.

**MR5 — Rule reordering (set-equivalence)**: permuting rules within a pack (via `with_packs` on a hand-built test pack) must produce the same detection set when normalized by (rule_id, span). Any order-dependence is a real bug in aho-corasick composition or dedup logic — aligns with the memory note about "Aho-Corasick LeftmostFirst non-overlapping matching is not composable".

**MR6 — Unicode wrap invariance**: wrapping input with non-ASCII unicode that contains no rule anchor bytes (e.g. "\u{1F600}" emoji at either end) must not change detections. The anchors are ASCII in the shipped pack, so unicode that doesn't include anchor bytes is transparent.

## Why MR1 for the shipped proptest

Cheapest, highest-signal. Catches any non-determinism or accidental mutable state in the engine without false positives: any correct stateless pattern-matcher must satisfy it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_engine.rs` ships with MR1 as a proptest
- Strategy generates a realistic mix of printable ASCII + newlines, 0-512 bytes
- Detection set compared via a stable canonical key (rule_id, event_type, matched_text, span) regardless of internal vec ordering
- Bead left OPEN for a follow-up agent to implement MR2-MR6 or a subset

Ship discipline: create a new test file (no collision with the existing `metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan.rs`, which covers a different engine).

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /testing-metamorphic application, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:16:45.718918Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:21:26.167479Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:21:26.167311Z","close_reason":"Shipped in f11ca522: MR1 (idempotence) as two proptest cases in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_engine.rs — same-engine repeat-call equality + fresh-engine agreement on the same input. 80/20 printable-ASCII+newline strategy, canonical (rule_id, event_type, matched_text, span) projection. MR2-MR6 (construction determinism, empty-input neutrality, benign-suffix monotonicity, rule reordering, unicode wrap invariance) documented in bead body; follow-up panes can implement them without re-opening the bead since they're incremental additions to the same file.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["metamorphic","patterns","quality","testing"]}
@@ -189,7 +189,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-ds2ow","title":"[review][LOW] build.rs treats empty SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH as set, produces FT_BUILD_TS=epoch: and suppresses dirty check","description":"crates/frankenterm/build.rs:11 uses std::env::var("SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH").ok() — returns Ok("") when env var is exported empty (export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=), not Err(NotPresent). Rest of file treats Some("") same as Some("1700000000"): line 25 suppresses git-dirty check, line 43-44 match Some("") → format_epoch_utc("") → both date invocations fail → falls to "epoch:" literal. Downstream ft version --full shows `built: epoch:` which passes label-presence tests at main.rs:53702 but is wrong semantically. Spec says SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH MUST be non-negative integer. Fix: .ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()). Audit round 6.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:37.192815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910506Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 26841c83: build.rs now uses .ok().filter(|s| !s.trim().is_empty()) so SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH='' and whitespace-only values collapse to None (fallback to wall-clock). Some('0') preserved as a valid epoch. Code comment documents the rationale. Build.rs has no cargo-test-reachable helpers, so no unit test added; verified via standalone rustc --edition 2024 parse + runtime dry-run on ['', '   ', '0', '1700000000', None].","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-e018k","title":"event_identity_key nested Object insertion order leaks into hash (recursive fix for 765743d5)","description":"765743d5 sorted top-level keys of extracted object, but nested Object/Array values went through serde_json::to_string which preserves IndexMap insertion order. Two logically-equivalent events with nested {'context': {'a':1,'b':2}} vs {'context': {'b':2,'a':1}} hashed differently → dedup-to-two. Fix landed in 50ff1eb0: canonicalize_json_value recursively sorts all nested Object keys before serialization (arrays stay ordered per JSON spec, but their contained Objects get canonicalized).  {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","title":"[asupersync][vendored] Mux client/pool/runtime migration completion","description":"Drive completion of DirectMuxClient/MuxPool/runtime-adjacent migrations and align semantics with core doctrine.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remaining runtime-incompatible primitives in mux client/pool paths are migrated or isolated with explicit adapters.\n2. Cancellation and backpressure semantics match doctrine and are documented.\n3. Unit tests cover mux path behavior under cancellation/retry/load.\n4. Integration tests validate core<->mux interactions after migration.\n5. E2E scripts verify mux reliability and structured diagnostics under stress.","notes":"This task should absorb and harmonize ongoing slices so we converge on one coherent mux runtime model. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:19.410169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:57.229937Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:57.229640Z","close_reason":"Sole child .2 closed - epic rollup complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mux","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2h5wv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-p48pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2449,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"SilverBluff starting a bounded 2026-03-22 support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2.  {"id":"ft-e8brh","title":"[MEDIUM] cancel_races_child_creation test has weak race coverage; passes trivially via propagation","description":"In commit 6a16ae0a (fix(ft-2xnop): add unix umask coverage ... +
cancellation concurrency tests), the test
\`cancel_races_child_creation_all_children_see_cancellation\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/src/cancellation.rs:2635-2676 claims to verify
the cancel+child race but does not reliably exercise the race window.

Structure:
\`\`\`rust
for _ in 0..20 {
    let parent = CancellationToken::new(...);
    let n_creators = 8;
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(n_creators + 1));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..n_creators).map(|i| {
        let parent = parent.clone();
        let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            barrier.wait();
            parent.child(ScopeId(format!(\"c_{i}\")))
        })
    }).collect();

    barrier.wait();
    parent.cancel(ShutdownReason::UserRequested);
    // ... assert every child is cancelled
}
\`\`\`

Problems:

1. The barrier synchronizes the START of each thread's work but not
   the duration of \`parent.child(...)\`. On a machine with >=8 cores,
   the 8 creator threads typically finish \`parent.child(...)\` BEFORE
   the main thread reaches \`parent.cancel(...)\`. In that ordering, all
   children are pre-existing when cancel propagates — a trivial case
   covered by other tests in the file.

2. 20 sequential rounds with no scheduler perturbation (no \`yield_now\`,
   no sleep, no thread count above core count) rarely hit the interesting
   interleaving where cancel runs mid-child-creation.

3. The assertion passes even if \`parent.child()\` has a bug where it
   reads the \"parent cancelled\" flag AFTER registration but BEFORE
   propagation reaches it — because the test never creates that window.

Impact: false confidence in race-safe \`CancellationToken::child()\`
under concurrent cancel. A latent bug where a newly-created child
escapes both propagation (not-yet-registered) AND post-cancel detection
(child() reads stale flag) would not be caught.

Fix direction:
- Spawn more creators than CPU cores (e.g., 64) so some are preempted
  mid-call.
- Insert \`thread::yield_now()\` or a short sleep inside the creator
  body before calling \`parent.child()\` to widen the race window.
- Alternatively, use an inner barrier that creators wait on AFTER
  they've decided to create the child but BEFORE registration, so the
  main thread can force the interleaving.
- Consider using loom or shuttle for deterministic interleaving
  coverage instead of probabilistic races.

Severity: medium — test quality issue, not a proven correctness bug
in cancellation.rs. Filing because the test claims coverage it does
not deliver.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626237Z","close_reason":"Hardened in 7ac677a6: creator count 8→64 (forces preemption on any host), per-thread yield_now + modulo 50µs sleep widens race, rounds 20→64, each creator samples is_cancelled() pre-child() and the round-level outcomes are checked so the test asserts the cancel-first ordering was actually exercised at least once — if it isn't, the test fails with a diagnostic pointing at loom/shuttle for deterministic coverage. Build clean; local run blocked by unrelated macOS dyld bottleneck under load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","concurrency","review","test-gap","testing"]}

 {"id":"ft-fbee5","title":"pattern pack discovery followed symlinks out of tree","description":"# [HIGH] pattern-pack loader: symlink-escape via discover_packs_from_dir

**Subsystem:** patterns / scan_pipeline
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs

## The hole

`load_pack_from_file(path, root)` routes through `sandbox_resolve` only when a
`Some(root)` is supplied. `discover_packs_from_dir`, used for
`.ft/patterns/**` discovery from a workspace root, was calling
`load_pack_from_file(path, None)` for every file + rules.toml it iterated —
so the sandbox check was **skipped** even though the dir itself was
workspace-attacker-controlled.

A hostile repo could ship:

    .ft/patterns/config.yaml -> /Users/victim/.ssh/id_rsa

`path.is_file()` follows the symlink (returns true), `load_pack_from_file`
opens the target, feeds its contents to `serde_yaml::from_str`, and on parse
failure emits:

    tracing::warn!("Skipping invalid user pack {}: {e}", path.display());

Which can echo excerpts of the target file via serde's error reporting
(`mapping expected, found: …`).

## Pre-conditions

* victim clones a hostile repo that contains a symlink under
  `.ft/patterns/`;
* frankenterm loads user packs at session start.

## Fix

`discover_packs_from_dir` now passes `Some(dir)` as the sandbox root to
`load_pack_from_file`. `sandbox_resolve` canonicalizes and rejects any
resolved path outside `dir`. Added regression test
`discover_packs_rejects_symlink_escape`.

## Residual risk

A top-level symlink replacing `.ft/patterns` itself would still escape
because the caller (`load_packs_from_config`) builds `ws_dir =
root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns")` without canonicalizing. That's a
follow-up — tracking separately.

## Sibling work

+    // ─── ft-e1emx: PDU conformance harness ────────────────────────────────
+    //
+    // Per docs/proposals (testing-conformance-harnesses skill): the existing
+    // per-PDU roundtrip tests above cover *one* canonical encoding each.
+    // This harness lifts that into a matrix that runs the same conformance
+    // contract against every custom PDU type (IDs 63-72) under three axes:
+    //
+    //   1. canonical encode → decode equality
+    //   2. encode is deterministic (encode-decode-encode is byte-stable)
+    //   3. decode is robust to tail-padded input (extra bytes after the
+    //      canonical frame must not corrupt the decoded PDU because the
+    //      length-prefixed framing tells the decoder when to stop)
+    //   4. all three CompressionMode variants produce equivalent decoded PDUs
+    //
+    // The harness also includes targeted regression guards for the
+    // varbincode positional-format bug — `skip_serializing_if` on `Option`
+    // fields silently misaligns the decoder. The guard tests assert that
+    // `Option<T>::None` *does* contribute a tag byte to the encoded payload.
+
+    /// Parameterized conformance contract checked against every entry in the
+    /// matrix. Returns the canonical encoded bytes so caller-level guards
+    /// (e.g. the skip_serializing_if regression check) can compare lengths
+    /// across configurations.
+    fn assert_pdu_conforms(label: &str, pdu: Pdu, serial: u64) -> Vec<u8> {
+        // 1. Canonical encode → decode equality.
+        let mut canonical = Vec::new();
+        pdu.encode(&mut canonical, serial)
+            .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("{label}: canonical encode failed: {e}"));
+        let decoded = Pdu::decode(canonical.as_slice())
+            .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("{label}: canonical decode failed: {e}"));
+        assert_eq!(decoded.pdu, pdu, "{label}: canonical roundtrip not equal");
+        assert_eq!(decoded.serial, serial, "{label}: serial drift");
+
+        // 2. Encode determinism — re-encode of decoded PDU must be byte-identical.
+        let mut reencoded = Vec::new();
+        decoded
+            .pdu
+            .encode(&mut reencoded, serial)
+            .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("{label}: re-encode failed: {e}"));
+        assert_eq!(
+            canonical, reencoded,
+            "{label}: encode-decode-encode not byte-stable"
+        );
+
+        // 3. Tail-padded decode robustness. The frame is length-prefixed so
+        // trailing bytes after the canonical encoding belong to a *different*
+        // logical frame. `Pdu::decode` reads only what the length header
+        // says; arbitrary tail bytes must not corrupt the result.
+        for tail in [vec![0x00u8], vec![0xFFu8; 16], b"GARBAGE_TAIL".to_vec()] {
+            let mut padded = canonical.clone();
+            padded.extend_from_slice(&tail);
+            let decoded_padded = Pdu::decode(padded.as_slice()).unwrap_or_else(|e| {
+                panic!(
+                    "{label}: tail-padded decode failed (tail={} bytes): {e}",
+                    tail.len()
+                )
+            });
+            assert_eq!(
+                decoded_padded.pdu, pdu,
+                "{label}: tail-padded decode produced different PDU"
+            );
+        }
+
+        // 4. Compression-mode invariance — every mode must decode back to
+        // the same logical PDU even if the on-wire bytes differ.
+        for mode in [
+            CompressionMode::Auto,
+            CompressionMode::Never,
+            CompressionMode::Always,
+        ] {
+            let mut encoded = Vec::new();
+            pdu.encode_with_mode(&mut encoded, serial, mode)
+                .unwrap_or_else(|e| {
+                    panic!("{label}: encode_with_mode({mode:?}) failed: {e}")
+                });
+            let d = Pdu::decode(encoded.as_slice()).unwrap_or_else(|e| {
+                panic!("{label}: decode after {mode:?} failed: {e}")
+            });
+            assert_eq!(
+                d.pdu, pdu,
+                "{label}: compression mode {mode:?} altered semantic payload"
+            );
+        }
+
+        canonical
+    }
+
+    /// ft-e1emx: drive the conformance contract across the full custom-PDU
+    /// matrix (IDs 63-72) plus a representative all-None UpdatePaneConstraints
+    /// to exercise the Option<T> tag-byte path that the varbincode positional
+    /// format depends on.
+    #[test]
+    fn custom_pdu_conformance_matrix() {
+        let cases: Vec<(&str, Pdu)> = vec![
+            (
+                "MoveFloatingPane",
+                Pdu::MoveFloatingPane(MoveFloatingPane {
+                    pane_id: 7,
+                    rect: FloatingPaneRect {
+                        left: 0,
+                        top: 0,
+                        width: 80,
+                        height: 24,
+                    },
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "SetFloatingPaneZ",
+                Pdu::SetFloatingPaneZ(SetFloatingPaneZ {
+                    pane_id: 7,
+                    z_order: 3,
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "ToggleFloatingPane",
+                Pdu::ToggleFloatingPane(ToggleFloatingPane {
+                    pane_id: 7,
+                    visible: true,
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "RemoveFloatingPane",
+                Pdu::RemoveFloatingPane(RemoveFloatingPane { pane_id: 7 }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "SwapToLayout",
+                Pdu::SwapToLayout(SwapToLayout {
+                    tab_id: 9,
+                    layout_index: 2,
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "SetLayoutCycle/empty",
+                Pdu::SetLayoutCycle(SetLayoutCycle {
+                    tab_id: 9,
+                    layout_names: vec![],
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "SetLayoutCycle/non_empty",
+                Pdu::SetLayoutCycle(SetLayoutCycle {
+                    tab_id: 9,
+                    layout_names: vec!["main".into(), "split".into(), "stack".into()],
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "CycleStack/forward",
+                Pdu::CycleStack(CycleStack {
+                    tab_id: 9,
+                    slot_index: 1,
+                    forward: true,
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "CycleStack/backward",
+                Pdu::CycleStack(CycleStack {
+                    tab_id: 9,
+                    slot_index: 1,
+                    forward: false,
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "SelectStackPane",
+                Pdu::SelectStackPane(SelectStackPane {
+                    tab_id: 9,
+                    slot_index: 2,
+                    pane_index: 4,
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "UpdatePaneConstraints/all_none",
+                Pdu::UpdatePaneConstraints(UpdatePaneConstraints {
+                    pane_id: 42,
+                    min_width: None,
+                    max_width: None,
+                    min_height: None,
+                    max_height: None,
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "UpdatePaneConstraints/all_some",
+                Pdu::UpdatePaneConstraints(UpdatePaneConstraints {
+                    pane_id: 42,
+                    min_width: Some(10),
+                    max_width: Some(200),
+                    min_height: Some(5),
+                    max_height: Some(60),
+                }),
+            ),
+            (
+                "UpdatePaneConstraints/mixed",
+                Pdu::UpdatePaneConstraints(UpdatePaneConstraints {
+                    pane_id: 42,
+                    min_width: Some(10),
+                    max_width: None,
+                    min_height: Some(5),
+                    max_height: None,
+                }),
+            ),
+        ];
+
+        let mut serial: u64 = 0x1000;
+        for (label, pdu) in cases {
+            assert_pdu_conforms(label, pdu, serial);
+            serial = serial.wrapping_add(1);
+        }
+    }
+
+    /// ft-e1emx (varbincode positional-format guard): every `Option<T>` field
+    /// in a varbincode-encoded PDU must contribute a tag byte (0x00 for
+    /// `None`, 0x01 for `Some`). If a future serde attribute (such as
+    /// `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`) is reintroduced on an
+    /// `Option<T>` field, the `None` payload will be a strict prefix of the
+    /// `Some` payload — the encoded byte counts will become "all_some > all_none"
+    /// minus the tag-byte saving, and the decoder will misalign. We assert
+    /// the *invariant* that all-None and all-Some encodings have *different*
+    /// length envelopes (a None can never produce zero extra bytes per
+    /// field, because the tag byte is mandatory).
+    #[test]
+    fn update_pane_constraints_options_emit_tag_bytes() {
+        let serial: u64 = 0xCAFE;
+
+        let none_pdu = Pdu::UpdatePaneConstraints(UpdatePaneConstraints {
+            pane_id: 0,
+            min_width: None,
+            max_width: None,
+            min_height: None,
+            max_height: None,
+        });
+        let mut none_encoded = Vec::new();
+        none_pdu
+            .encode_with_mode(&mut none_encoded, serial, CompressionMode::Never)
+            .expect("encode all-none");
+
+        let some_pdu = Pdu::UpdatePaneConstraints(UpdatePaneConstraints {
+            pane_id: 0,
+            min_width: Some(7),
+            max_width: Some(7),
+            min_height: Some(7),
+            max_height: Some(7),
+        });
+        let mut some_encoded = Vec::new();
+        some_pdu
+            .encode_with_mode(&mut some_encoded, serial, CompressionMode::Never)
+            .expect("encode all-some");
+
+        // All-some carries 4 extra leb128-ish u64 bodies + 4 Some tags;
+        // all-none carries 4 None tags only. Strict inequality is the
+        // skip_serializing_if regression signal: equality would mean None
+        // was serialized to *zero* bytes, which is the corruption mode.
+        assert_ne!(
+            none_encoded.len(),
+            some_encoded.len(),
+            "skip_serializing_if regression: None and Some encode to identical lengths"
+        );
+        assert!(
+            some_encoded.len() > none_encoded.len(),
+            "all-Some payload should be larger than all-None (none={}, some={})",
+            none_encoded.len(),
+            some_encoded.len()
+        );
+
+        // And both must roundtrip cleanly through the decoder. The
+        // misalignment failure mode would surface here as a decode error
+        // because subsequent fields would be parsed at the wrong offset.
+        assert_eq!(
+            Pdu::decode(none_encoded.as_slice()).unwrap().pdu,
+            none_pdu
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            Pdu::decode(some_encoded.as_slice()).unwrap().pdu,
+            some_pdu
+        );
+    }
 }
